abandonner.of: Kar chascun jur de mort [il] s'abandunet.
abandonner.tr: For each day he gives himself up to death.

abandonner.of: Mais de s'espee ne volt mie guerpir;
abandonner.tr: But he would by no means give up his sword;

abandonner.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
abandonner.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

abattre.of: Od ses cadables les turs en abatied.
abattre.tr: With his siege engines he beats down its towers.

absoudre.of: De sa main destre l'ad asols e seignet,
absoudre.tr: With his right hand he absolved and blessed him,

accabler.tr: «Hear, my lords, what calamity weighs upon us:

accomplir.tr: I will fulfill your command.» AOI.

accomplir.of: Par quele gent quiet il espleiter tant?»
accomplir.tr: With what people does he think to achieve so much?»

accorder.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
accorder.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

acheminer.of: Entret en sa veie, si s'est achiminez. AOI.
acheminer.tr: He sets out on his way, and thus has started off. AOI.

acheminer.of: Vers dulce France tuit sunt achiminez. AOI.
acheminer.tr: Toward sweet France they have all set out. AOI.

acquitter.of: Se de mun cors voeil aquiter la vie,
acquitter.tr: If I wish to ransom the life of my body,

acquitter.of: Que as espees ne seit einz eslegiet.»
acquitter.tr: That shall not first be paid for at the point of swords.»

adorer.tr: the glorious, whom we must adore!
The Glorious One, whom we must adore!

affiner.of: Tant i avrat de besanz esmerez
affiner.tr: there will be so much of it in refined bezants

agir.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
agir.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

aider.of: Prozdom i out pur sun seignur aider;
aider.tr: A worthy man, and there he was to aid his lord;

aider.of: E lui aidez e pur seignur le tenez.»
aider.tr: And help him and hold him as your lord.»

aider.of: Que (v)[n]os aidez de Rollant le barun,
aider.tr: That you help us with Roland the baron,

aider.of: Si nos aidez de Rollant li marchis,
aider.tr: Thus help us regarding Roland the marquis,

aimer.of: Li reis Marsilie la tient, ki Deu nen amet,
aimer.tr: King Marsile holds it — he who does not love God,

aimer.of: E dit al cunte: «Jo ne vus aim nient;
aimer.tr: and he says to the count: «I do not love you at all;

aimer.of: Ne l'amerai a trestut mun vivant,
aimer.tr: I shall not love him all the days of my life,

aimer.of: Li duze per, por [ço] qu'il l'aiment tant,
aimer.tr: The twelve peers, because they love him so much,

aimer.of: Il l'a[i]ment tant ne li faldrunt nient;
aimer.tr: They love him so much they will not fail him at all;
Otherwise he will not love me at all.»

aimer.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
aimer.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

aimer.of: Li .XII. per, que Carles aimet tant,
aimer.tr: The Twelve Peers, whom Charles loves so much,

aimer.of: «Jo vos aim mult, sire,» dist ele al cunte,
aimer.tr: «I love you greatly, sire,» she said to the count,

aller.of: Alez en est en un verger suz l'umbre;
aller.tr: He has gone off into an orchard beneath the shade;

aller.of: Francs s'en irunt en France la lur tere.
aller.tr: The Franks will go away to France, their own land.

aller.of: «Seignurs baruns, a Carlemagnes irez;
aller.tr: «Lord barons, you shall go to Charlemagne;

aller.of: Dist a ses humes: «Seignurs, vos en ireiz;
aller.tr: He said to his men: «Lords, you shall go forth;

aller.of: Desuz un pin en est li reis alez,
aller.tr: Beneath a pine the king has gone,

aller.of: Li emper[er]es s'en vait desuz un pin.
aller.tr: The emperor goes off beneath a pine tree.

aller.of: Mais il me mandet que en France m'en alge:
aller.tr: But he commands me that I depart from France:

aller.of: Respunt dux Neimes: «Jo irai par vostre dun!
aller.tr: Duke Naimes replies: «I will go, by your leave!

aller.of: Vos n'irez pas uan de mei si luign.
aller.tr: You shall not go this year so far from me.

aller.of: Alez sedeir, quant nuls ne vos sumunt.»
aller.tr: Go sit down, since no one summons you.»

aller.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
aller.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

aller.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
aller.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

aller.of: E jo irai al Sarazin en Espaigne,
aller.tr: And I shall go to the Saracen in Spain,

aller.of: Sin vois vedeir alques de sun semblant.»
aller.tr: And go see something of his disposition.»

aller.of: «Alez sedeir desur cel palie blanc!
aller.tr: «Go sit down upon that white silk cloth!

aller.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
aller.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

aller.of: Guenes respunt: «Pur mei n'iras tu mie!
aller.tr: Ganelon answers: «You will not go at all on my behalf!

aller.of: En Sarraguce en irai a Marsilie;
aller.tr: To Saragossa I shall go, to Marsile;

aller.of: «En Sarraguce sai ben, [qu']aler m'estoet.
aller.tr: «I know well that I must go to Saragossa.

aller.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
aller.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

aller.of: Puisquel comant, aler vus en estoet.»
aller.tr: Since I command it, you must go.»

aller.of: Or irez vos certes, quant jol cumant.»
aller.tr: Now you will go indeed, since I command it.»

aller.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
aller.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

aller.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
aller.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

aller.of: Guenes li quens s'en vait a sun ostel,
aller.tr: Ganelon the count goes off to his lodging,

aller.of: Ki ço jugat, que doüsez aler
aller.tr: He who decreed that you should go

aller.of: En dulce France, seignurs, vos en irez:
aller.tr: To sweet France, lords, you will go back:

aller.of: Vait s'apuier suz le pin a la tige.
aller.tr: He goes to lean back against the trunk of the pine.

aller.of: Enz el verger s'en est alez li reis,
aller.tr: Into the orchard the king has gone,

aller.of: Par tantes teres est alet cunquerant,
aller.tr: Through so many lands has he gone conquering,

aller.of: Icil en vait al rei Marsiliun;
aller.tr: He goes to the king Marsile;

amener.of: Dis blanches mules fist amener Marsilies,
amener.tr: Marsile had ten white mules brought,

amener.of: Al siege ad Ais en serez amenet,
amener.tr: To the seat at Aix you will be brought,

amener.of: Ço dist li reis: «E vos l'i ameneiz.»
amener.tr: Thus said the king: «And you, bring him here to me.»

amener.of: Mult grant aveir vos en faz amener,
amener.tr: Very great treasure I have brought to you,

amener.of: Se il fust vif, jo l'oüsse amenet.
amener.tr: Had he been alive, I would have brought him.

amuser.of: As tables juent pur els esbaneier
amuser.tr: At backgammon they play to amuse themselves

annoncer.tr: They announced to you these very words.

apaiser.of: Franceis se taisent: as les vus aquisez.
apaiser.tr: The Franks fall silent: behold them quieted.

apparaître.of: Tresvait la noit, e apert la clere albe.
apparaître.tr: The night passes, and the bright dawn appears.

appeler.of: Il en apelet e ses dux e ses cuntes:
appeler.tr: He calls forth both his dukes and his counts:
So he summoned Clarin (...) of Balaguer,

appeler.of: Des plus feluns dís en ad apelez:
appeler.tr: Of the most felonious he has summoned ten of them:

appeler.of: Noble vassal vos i solt hom clamer.
appeler.tr: A noble vassal one was wont to call you there.

appeler.of: Li reis apelet Malduit sun tresorer:
appeler.tr: The king calls Malduit, his treasurer:

apporter.of: De Sarraguce ci vos aport les clefs;
apporter.tr: From Saragossa here I bring you the keys;

approcher.of: Envers le rei s'est Guenes aproismet,
approcher.tr: Toward the king Ganelon has drawn near,
The emperor approaches his homeward way.

appuyer.of: Vait s'apuier suz le pin a la tige.
appuyer.tr: He goes to lean back against the trunk of the pine.

arracher.of: Par tel air l'at estrussee e brandie,
arracher.tr: With such force he wrenched and brandished it,

arranger.of: Si duist sa barbe, afaitad sun gernun,
arranger.tr: He smoothed his beard, arranged his mustache,

arriver.of: De cest message nos avendrat grant perte.»
arriver.tr: From this mission great loss will befall us.»

assaillir.of: Sun cors demenie mult fierement asalt.
assaillir.tr: Very fiercely it attacks his own body.

assembler.tr: He has joined the Saracen messengers;

assembler.of: N'asemblereit jamais Carles si grant esforz;
assembler.tr: Charles would never again assemble so great a host;

asseoir.of: Sur palies blancs siedent cil cevaler,
asseoir.tr: Upon white silks those knights are seated,

asseoir.of: La siet li reis, ki dulce france tient.
asseoir.tr: There sits the king who holds sweet France.

asseoir.of: Alez sedeir, quant nuls ne vos sumunt.»
asseoir.tr: Go sit down, since no one summons you.»
«Go sit down upon that white silk cloth!

asseoir.of: Er matin sedeit li emperere suz l'umbre;
asseoir.tr: Yesterday morning the emperor sat in the shade;
For Marsile has seated himself on the throne.

assiéger.tr: He will come to besiege you in Saragossa;

attarder.of: Mais Blancandrins ki envers lu s'atarget;
attarder.tr: But Blancandrin, who lingers back toward him —

atteindre.of: Nes poet guarder que mals ne l'i ateignet. AOI.
atteindre.tr: he cannot keep harm from reaching him there. AOI.

attendre.of: De Guenelun atent li reis nuveles,
attendre.tr: From Ganelon the king awaits news,
four hundred thousand await the daybreak.

avoir.of: set anz tuz pleins ad estet en Espaigne:
avoir.tr: has been in Spain seven full years:

avoir.of: N'i ad castel ki devant lui remaigne;
avoir.tr: There is no fortress that stands against him;

avoir.of: Jo nen ai ost qui bataille li dunne,
avoir.tr: I have no army that may give him battle,

avoir.of: Ne n'ai tel gent ki la sue derumpet.
avoir.tr: Nor have I such a host as might break his.

avoir.of: N'i ad paien ki un sul mot respundet,
avoir.tr: There is no pagan who answers a single word,

avoir.of: Prozdom i out pur sun seignur aider;
avoir.tr: A worthy man, and there he was to aid his lord;

avoir.of: En ceste tere ad asez osteiet;
avoir.tr: In this land he has campaigned long enough;

avoir.of: Ne nus aiuns les mals ne les suffraites.»
avoir.tr: And that we have the woes and the privations.»

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie out sun cunseill finet:
avoir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council:

avoir.of: Des plus feluns dís en ad apelez:
avoir.tr: Of the most felonious he has summoned ten of them:

avoir.of: Dient paien: «De ço avun nus asez!» AOI.
avoir.tr: The pagans say: «Of this we have enough!» AOI.

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilie out finet sun cunseill;
avoir.tr: King Marsile had ended his council;

avoir.of: Pur le soen Deu qu'il ait m(er)ercit de mei.
avoir.tr: For the sake of his God, that he have mercy on me.

avoir.of: S'il voelt ostages, il en avrat par veir.»
avoir.tr: If he wants hostages, he shall have them indeed.»

avoir.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult bon plait en avreiz.» AOI.
avoir.tr: Blancandrin said: «You shall have a very good agreement out of it.» AOI.

avoir.of: Vindrent a Charles ki France ad en baillie:
avoir.tr: They came to Charles, who has France in his keeping:

avoir.of: Cordres ad prise e les murs peceiez,
avoir.tr: He has taken Cordres and shattered its walls,

avoir.of: Mult grant eschech en unt si chevaler
avoir.tr: His knights take very great booty from it

avoir.of: En la citet nen ad remes paien,
avoir.tr: In the city there remains no pagan,

avoir.of: La u cist furent, des altres i out bien:
avoir.tr: Where these were, there were indeed many others:

avoir.of: De dulce france i ad quinze milliers.
avoir.tr: From sweet France there are fifteen thousand.

avoir.of: Un faldestoed i unt fait tut d or mer,
avoir.tr: A throne they have made all of pure gold,

avoir.of: Blanche ad la barbe e tut flurit le chef,
avoir.tr: White is his beard and all blossomed his head,

avoir.of: Gent ad le cors e le cuntenant fier,
avoir.tr: Noble is his body and his bearing proud,

avoir.of: Blancandrins ad tut premereins parled,
avoir.tr: Blancandrin spoke first of all,

avoir.of: Enquis ad mult la lei de salvetez;
avoir.tr: he has inquired much into the law of salvation;

avoir.of: Tant i avrat de besanz esmerez
avoir.tr: there will be so much of it in refined bezants

avoir.of: En cest païs avez estet asez;
avoir.tr: In this land you have stayed long enough;

avoir.of: Quant se redrecet, mult par out fier lu vis;
avoir.tr: When he straightens up, his face looked most proud;

avoir.of: Dist as messages: «Vus avez mult ben dit.
avoir.tr: He said to the messengers: «You have spoken very well.

avoir.of: De cez paroles que vos avez ci dit,
avoir.tr: Concerning these words that you have spoken here,

avoir.of: «Dunt vos avrez ú dis ú quinze ú vint.
avoir.tr: «Of whom you shall have ten or fifteen or twenty.

avoir.of: E sin avrez, ço quid, de plus gentilz.
avoir.tr: And you shall have, I think, [some] of more noble birth.

avoir.of: Les dis messages ad fait enz hosteler;
avoir.tr: The ten messengers he has had lodged within;

avoir.of: .XII. serjanz les unt ben cunreez.
avoir.tr: Twelve sergeants have well equipped them.

avoir.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
avoir.tr: Mass and matins the king has heard.

avoir.of: Des Francs de France en i ad plus de mil.
avoir.tr: Of the Franks of France there are more than a thousand of them.

avoir.of: «Li reis Marsilie m'ad tramis ses messages;
avoir.tr: «King Marsile has sent me his messengers;

avoir.of: Li empereres out sa raisun fenie.
avoir.tr: The emperor had finished his speech.

avoir.of: Set anz [ad] pleins, que en Espaigne venimes;
avoir.tr: Seven full years it is, since we came into Spain;

avoir.of: Pris ai Valterne e la tere de Pine
avoir.tr: I have taken Valterne and the land of Pine

avoir.of: Faites la guer[re] cum vos l'avez enprise:
avoir.tr: Wage the war as you have undertaken it:

avoir.of: Meillor vassal n'aveit en la curt nul,
avoir.tr: There was no better vassal in the court,

avoir.of: E dist al rei: «Ben l'avez entendud,
avoir.tr: And he said to the king: «You have indeed heard it,

avoir.of: Guenes li quens ço vus ad respondud,
avoir.tr: Ganelon the count has answered you thus,

avoir.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
avoir.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

avoir.of: Vos li avez tuz ses castels toluz,
avoir.tr: You have taken from him all his castles,

avoir.of: Od voz caables avez fruiset ses murs,
avoir.tr: With your siege engines you have shattered his walls,

avoir.of: Quant il vos mandet, qu'aiez mercit de lui,
avoir.tr: When he sends word to you, that you have mercy on him,

avoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben ad parlet li dux.» AOI.
avoir.tr: The Franks say: «The duke has spoken well.» AOI.

avoir.of: En cest païs avez estet set anz;
avoir.tr: In this land you have been seven years;

avoir.of: Mult unt oüd e peines e ahans.
avoir.tr: Much have they endured, both hardships and toils.

avoir.of: Vairs out [les oilz] e mult fier lu visage,
avoir.tr: He had bright eyes and a very fierce face,

avoir.of: Gent out le cors e les costez out larges;
avoir.tr: He had a noble body and his sides were broad;

avoir.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
avoir.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

avoir.of: Ço set hom ben, n'ai cure de manace;
avoir.tr: One knows well, I have no care for a threat;

avoir.of: Dunc ad tel doel pur poi d'ire ne fent,
avoir.tr: then he has such grief that he nearly bursts from rage,

avoir.of: Sur mei avez turnet fals jugement.
avoir.tr: you have turned a false judgment upon me.

avoir.of: Ensurquetut si ai jo vostre soer,
avoir.tr: Above all, I have your sister,

avoir.of: Sin ai un filz, ja plus bels n'en estoet:
avoir.tr: and by her I have a son, there is none more handsome:

avoir.of: Carles respunt: «trop avez tendre coer.
avoir.tr: Charles answers: «you have too tender a heart.

avoir.of: Oït l'avez, sur vos le jugent Franc.»
avoir.tr: You have heard it, the Franks judge it against you.»

avoir.of: - «Sire,» dist Guenes, «ço ad tut fait Rollant!
avoir.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «Roland has done all this!

avoir.of: Ço dist li reis: «Trop avez maltalant.
avoir.tr: The king said: «You have too much ill will.

avoir.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
avoir.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

avoir.of: Nu l'out Basilies ne sis freres Basant.»
avoir.tr: Basil had none of it, nor his brother Basant.»

avoir.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
avoir.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

avoir.of: De sa main destre l'ad asols e seignet,
avoir.tr: With his right hand he absolved and blessed him,

avoir.of: Esperuns d'or ad en ses piez fermez,
avoir.tr: He has fastened golden spurs on his feet,

avoir.of: En (la) cort al rei mult i avez ested,
avoir.tr: In the king's court you have long been,

avoir.of: Ne mes Rollant, ki uncore en avrat hunte.
avoir.tr: Save Roland, who will yet have shame from it.

avoir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
avoir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

avoir.of: E out predet dejuste Carcasonie;
avoir.tr: And had taken booty near Carcassonne;

avoir.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
avoir.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

avoir.of: L'emperere meïsmes ad tut a sun talent.
avoir.tr: The emperor himself has everything to his liking.

avoir.of: Un faldestoet out suz l'umbre d'un pin;
avoir.tr: There was a throne in the shade of a pine;

avoir.of: N'i ad celoi ki mot sunt ne mot tint,
avoir.tr: There is none among them who speaks a word nor utters a word,

avoir.of: Par lui orrez si avrez pais u nun.»
avoir.tr: From him you will hear whether you will have peace or not.»

avoir.of: Li reis Marsilies ad la culur muee;
avoir.tr: King Marsile has changed color;

avoir.of: De sun algeir ad la hanste crollee.
avoir.tr: With his javelin he has shaken the shaft.

avoir.of: Cuntre dous deie l'ad del furrer getee,
avoir.tr: He drew it two fingers' width out of the scabbard,

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «Mult estes bele e clere!
avoir.tr: And said to it: «You are very fair and bright!

avoir.of: Tant vus avrai en curt a rei portee!
avoir.tr: So long shall I have borne you at the king's court!

avoir.of: Einz vos avrunt li meillor comperee.»
avoir.tr: Before the best men shall have paid dearly for you.»

avoir.of: Dist l'algalifes: «Mal nos avez baillit,
avoir.tr: The caliph said: «You have served us ill,

avoir.of: Que jo ne li die, se tant ai de leisir,
avoir.tr: From telling him, if I have so much leisure,

avoir.of: Dient paien: «Noble baron ad ci!» AOI.
avoir.tr: The pagans say: «Here is a noble baron!» AOI.

avoir.of: Si li ad dit: «A tort vos curuciez,
avoir.tr: And said to him: «Wrongly do you grow angry,

avoir.of: L'altre meitet avrat Rollant, sis nies:
avoir.tr: The other half Roland, his nephew, will have:

avoir.of: Mulz orguillos parçuner i avrez!
avoir.tr: A very proud co-sharer you will have there!

avoir.of: Vus n'i avrez palefreid ne destrer,
avoir.tr: You will have there neither palfrey nor warhorse,

avoir.of: El destre poign al paien l'ad liv(e)ret.
avoir.tr: Into the right fist of the pagan he handed it over.

avoir.of: Freint le seel, getet en ad la cire,
avoir.tr: He breaks the seal, has cast away the wax,

avoir.of: «Carle me mandet, ki France ad en baillie,
avoir.tr: «Charles, who has France in his keeping, commands me,

avoir.of: E dist al rei: «Guenes ad dit folie;
avoir.tr: And said to the king: «Ganelon has spoken folly;

avoir.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
avoir.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

avoir.of: Quant l'oït Guenes, l'espee en ad branlie;
avoir.tr: When Ganelon heard this, he brandished his sword;

avoir.of: De nostre prod m'ad plevie sa feid.»
avoir.tr: He has pledged me his faith concerning our advantage.»

avoir.of: E Guenes (l')ad pris par la main destre ad deiz,
avoir.tr: And Ganelon took him by the right hand by the fingers,

avoir.of: «Bel sire Guenes,» ço li ad dit Marsilie,
avoir.tr: «Fair sir Ganelon,» Marsile said to him,

avoir.of: «Jo vos ai fait alques de legerie,
avoir.tr: «I have done you something of a wrong,

avoir.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
avoir.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

avoir.of: Il est mult vielz, si ad sun tens uset;
avoir.tr: He is very old, and has worn out his time;

avoir.of: Men escient dous cenz anz ad passet.
avoir.tr: To my knowledge he has passed two hundred years.

avoir.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors demened,
avoir.tr: Through so many lands he has driven his body,

avoir.of: Tanz [colps] ad pris sur sun escut bucler,
avoir.tr: So many blows has he taken upon his buckler-shield,

avoir.of: Que plus n'i ad d'onur e de bontet.
avoir.tr: That there is not yet more of honor and goodness in him.

avoir.of: De tel barnage l'ad Deus enluminet,
avoir.tr: With such nobility has God illumined him,

avoir.of: Men escientre dous cenz anz ad e mielz.
avoir.tr: To my knowledge he is two hundred years old and more.

avoir.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors traveillet,
avoir.tr: Through so many lands he has worn out his body,

avoir.of: Tanz colz ad pris de lances e d'espiet,
avoir.tr: so many blows he has taken from lances and spears,

avoir.of: Tanz riches reis cunduiz a mendistiet:
avoir.tr: so many rich kings he has brought to beggary:

avoir.of: N'at tel vassal suz la cape del ciel.
avoir.tr: there is no such vassal under the cope of heaven.

avoir.of: Les .XII. pers, que Carles ad tant chers,
avoir.tr: The Twelve Peers, whom Charles holds so dear,

joindre.tr: That he will become, his hands joined, your vassal,

jouer.of: As tables juent pur els esbaneier
jouer.tr: At backgammon they play to amuse themselves

juger.of: Li duze per mar i serunt jugez!»
juger.tr: The twelve peers would be ill-judged [for it]!»

juger.of: Si as juget qu'a Marsiliun en alge!
juger.tr: So you have decided that I should go to Marsile!

juger.of: Oït l'avez, sur vos le jugent Franc.»
juger.tr: You have heard it, the Franks judge it against you.»

juger.of: Ki ço jugat, que doüsez aler
juger.tr: He who decreed that you should go

juger.of: Pois me jugez Rollant a rereguarde.
juger.tr: Then assign Roland to me for the rearguard.

juger.of: Kar me jugez, ki ert en la rereguarde.»
juger.tr: Now judge for me, who shall be in the rearguard.»

juger.of: Li quens Rollant quant il s'oït juger,
juger.tr: Count Roland, when he hears himself thus assigned,

juger.of: La rereguarde avez sur mei jugiet.
juger.tr: You have assigned the rearguard to me.

jurer.of: Que l'un a l'altre la sue feit plevit,
jurer.tr: That each pledged his faith to the other,

jurer.of: De nostre prod m'ad plevie sa feid.»
jurer.tr: He has pledged me his faith concerning our advantage.»

jurer.of: La traïsun me jurrez de Rollant si illi est.»
jurer.tr: You will swear to me the betrayal of Roland, if it is so.»

jurer.of: La traïsun jurat, e si s'en est forsfait. AOI.
jurer.tr: He swore the betrayal, and thus made himself guilty of it. AOI.

jurer.of: Ço ad juret li Sarrazins espans:
jurer.tr: The Spanish Saracen has sworn this:

lacer.of: Healmes lacez e ceintes lur espees,
lacer.tr: helmets laced and their swords girded,

laisser.of: Laissun les fols, as sages nus tenuns.» AOI.
laisser.tr: Let us leave the fools, let us hold to the wise.» AOI.

laisser.of: E dist al rei: «Laisez ester voz Francs!
laisser.tr: And said to the king: «Let your Franks be!

laisser.of: Se lui lessez, n'i trametrez plus saive.»
laisser.tr: If you leave him aside, you will not send a wiser one.»

laisser.of: A lui lais jo mes honurs e mes fieus.
laisser.tr: To him I leave my lands and my fiefs.

laisser.of: Jo ne lerreie, por l'or que Deus fist
laisser.tr: I would not refrain, for all the gold that God made

laisser.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
laisser.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.
His rearguard he will leave behind him:

lever.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
lever.tr: The emperor is risen at morning;

lever.of: Turpins de Reins en est levet del renc,
lever.tr: Turpin of Reims has risen from the ranks,

lever.of: Ambes ses mains en levat cuntre munt,
lever.tr: He raised both his hands toward the heavens,

lever.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
lever.tr: The emperor has risen at morning;

lever.of: En sur un tertre cuntre le ciel levee.
lever.tr: raised it up on a hilltop against the sky.

lier.of: Pris e liez serez par poested;
lier.tr: You will be seized and bound by force,

lier.of: Par poestet serez pris e liez;
lier.tr: By force you will be taken and bound;

lisser.of: Si duist sa barbe, afaitad sun gernun,
lisser.tr: He smoothed his beard, arranged his mustache,

lisser.of: Si duist sa barbe, e detoerst sun gernun;
lisser.tr: He smoothed his beard, and twisted his moustache;

livrer.of: Livrez m'en ore le guant e le bastun.»
livrer.tr: Hand me now the glove and the staff.»

livrer.of: Puis li livrat le bastun e le bref.
livrer.tr: Then he handed over to him the staff and the letter.

livrer.of: El destre poign al paien l'ad liv(e)ret.
livrer.tr: Into the right fist of the pagan he handed it over.

livrer.of: Livrez le mei, jo en ferai la justise.»
livrer.tr: Hand him over to me, I will do justice on him for it.»

livrer.of: Altre bataille lur livrez de meïsme:

livrer.tr: I will give him a mortal battle.»

louer.of: Ben en purrat luer ses soldeiers.
louer.tr: Well will he be able to pay his soldiers with it.

louer.of: Loat sun Deu, ne fist altre respuns.
louer.tr: Praised his God, made no other reply.

mander.of: Mandez Carlun a l'orguillus, (e) al fier,
mander.tr: Send word to Charles, to the proud, to the fierce,

mander.of: Iço vus mandet reis Marsilies, li bers:
mander.tr: This is what King Marsile, the baron, sends word to you:

mander.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill finer:
mander.tr: His barons he summons to settle his counsel:

mander.of: Ses baruns mandet pur sun cunseill fenir:
mander.tr: He summons his barons to settle his counsel:

mander.of: Mais il me mandet que en France m'en alge:
mander.tr: But he commands me that I depart from France:

mander.of: Quant ço vos mandet li reis Marsiliun,
mander.tr: When the king Marsile sends you word of this,

mander.of: Quant il vos mandet, qu'aiez mercit de lui,
mander.tr: When he sends word to you, that you have mercy on him,

mander.of: Iço vus mandet Carlemagnes, li ber,
mander.tr: This is what Charlemagne the baron commands you:

mander.of: Que Charles li mandet, li reis poesteïfs,
mander.tr: What Charles commands him, the mighty king,

mander.of: Par mei li mandet, sun mortel enemi.»
mander.tr: Commands him through me, his mortal enemy.»

mander.of: Quar ço vos mandet Carles, ki France tient,
mander.tr: For thus Charles, who rules France, commands you:

mander.of: «Carle me mandet, ki France ad en baillie,
mander.tr: «Charles, who has France in his keeping, commands me,

mander.of: E si vos mandet reis Marsilies li ber,
mander.tr: And so King Marsile the brave sends word to you,

mendier.of: Ne nus seiuns cunduiz a mendeier.» AOI.
mendier.tr: And that we be reduced to begging.» AOI.

mener.of: En Sarraguce menez vostre ost banie,
mener.tr: To Saragossa lead your summoned host,

mener.of: Enpres li dient: «Sire, car nos menez!»
mener.tr: Then they say to him: «Lord, do lead us along!»

mener.of: Menet serez . . . [tut] dreit ad Ais le siet:
mener.tr: You will be led straight to the seat of Aix:

mener.of: Enz el verger l'en meinet josqu'al rei.
mener.tr: Into the orchard he leads him as far as the king.

mettre.of: Li frein sunt d'or, les seles d'argent mises.
mettre.tr: The bridles are of gold, the saddles set with silver.

mettre.of: Pa[r] num de ocire i metrai un mien filz,
mettre.tr: At risk of being slain, I will put there a son of mine,

mettre.of: Metez le sege a tute vostre vie,
mettre.tr: Lay the siege for the whole of your life,

mettre.of: E tutes teres met en chalengement!
mettre.tr: And puts all lands into contention!

mettre.of: Or e argent lur met tant en present,
mettre.tr: He puts so much gold and silver before them as gifts,

mettre.of: Quant le vit Guenes, mist la main a l'espee,
mettre.tr: When Ganelon saw it, he put his hand to his sword,

mettre.of: Sa rereguarde avrat detres sei mise;
mettre.tr: He will have placed his rearguard behind him;

mettre.of: Il les ad prises, en sa hoese les butet, AOI.
mettre.tr: He took them, into his boot he stuffed them, AOI.

meurtrir.of: La gent de France iert blecee e blesmie;
meurtrir.tr: The host of France will be wounded and bruised;

monter.of: Cil sunt muntez ki le message firent,
monter.tr: Those who carried the message have mounted,

monter.of: Cunseill d orguill n'est dreiz que a plus munt,
monter.tr: Counsel of pride, it is not right that it rise further,

monter.of: Ceste grant guerre ne deit munter a plus.»
monter.tr: This great war ought not to mount up to more.»

monter.of: En Tachebrun, sun destrer est munted;
monter.tr: He has mounted Tachebrun, his warhorse;

monter.of: Pois est munted, entret en sun veiage. AOI.
monter.tr: Then he mounted, and set out on his journey. AOI.

montrer.of: Quant por ferir vus demustrai grant ire.
montrer.tr: when I showed you great anger by making to strike you.

mordre.of: El destre braz li morst uns vers si mals.
mordre.tr: In his right arm a fierce boar bit him.

mourir.of: Ne li chalt, sire, de quel mort nus muriuns.
mourir.tr: Cares not, sire, by what death we may die.

mourir.of: Mielz est que sul moerge que tant bon chevaler.
mourir.tr: Better that I alone die than so many good knights.

mourir.of: La murrez vus a hunte e a viltet.»
mourir.tr: There you will die in shame and in disgrace.»

mourir.of: Que suls i moerge en l'estrange cuntree,
mourir.tr: That I died alone in the foreign land,

mourir.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
mourir.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

mourir.of: Tanz riches reis morz e vencuz en champ:
mourir.tr: So many rich kings dead and vanquished in the field:

mourir.of: Mort sunt li cunte, se est ki mei en creit.
mourir.tr: The counts are dead, if there is anyone who believes me in this.

mourir.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
mourir.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

mourir.of: E, se il poet, murrat i veirement.
mourir.tr: And, if he can, he will truly die there.

muer.of: Set cenz cameilz e mil hosturs muez,
muer.tr: seven hundred camels and a thousand moulted hawks,

muer.of: Li reis Marsilies ad la culur muee;
Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
muer.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

naviguer.tr: Before they had sailed four leagues,

noyer.of: La sunt neiez, jamais nes en verrez;
noyer.tr: There they were drowned, never will you see them again;

octroyer.of: Li quens Rollant, ki ne l'otriet mie,
octroyer.tr: Count Roland, who did not agree to it at all,

octroyer.of: Se cest acorde ne vulez otrier,
octroyer.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

octroyer.of: Si ceste acorde ne volez otrier,
octroyer.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

oser.of: Que li Franceis asmastes a ferir;
oser.tr: In that you dared to strike the Frenchman;

parler.of: Sa custume est qu'il parolet a leisír.
parler.tr: His custom is that he speaks at leisure.

parler.of: Dient Franceis: «Ben ad parlet li dux.» AOI.
parler.tr: The Franks say: «The duke has spoken well.» AOI.

parler.of: N'en parlez mais, se jo nel vos cumant!» AOI.
parler.tr: Speak of it no more, unless I command you to!» AOI.

parler.of: Par grant saveir parolet li uns a l'altre.
parler.tr: With great wisdom the one speaks to the other.

parler.of: Par grant saver cumencet a parler
parler.tr: With great wisdom he begins to speak,

parler.of: Apres parlat ses filz envers Marsilies,
parler.tr: After this his son spoke to Marsile,

parler.of: De Carlemagne vos voeill oïr parler.
parler.tr: I want to hear you speak of Charlemagne.

parler.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Qu'en parlereient il plus?
parler.tr: Marsile said: «Why should they speak further about it?

parler.of: Par grant veisdie cumencet a parler,
parler.tr: With great cunning he begins to speak,

parler.of: Dunc ad parled a lei de chevaler:
parler.tr: Then spoke as befits a knight:
Angrily he spoke to his stepfather:

passer.of: Vendrat li jurz, si passerat li termes,
passer.tr: The day will come, and so the term will pass,

passer.of: Ja einz ne verrat passer cest premer meis,
passer.tr: He will not see this first month pass

passer.of: Vers Engletere passat il la mer salse,
passer.tr: Toward England he crossed the salt sea,

passer.of: Men escient dous cenz anz ad passet.
passer.tr: To my knowledge he has passed two hundred years.

passer.of: Ja ne verrez cest premer meis passet
passer.tr: Never will you see this first month pass

passer.of: Tresvait le jur, la noit est aserie.
passer.tr: The day passes, the night falls dark.

passer.of: Tresvait la noit, e apert la clere albe.
passer.tr: The night passes, and the bright dawn appears.

payer.of: Dunt bien purrez voz soldeiers luer.
payer.tr: that you will well be able to pay your soldiers.

penser.of: Baisset sun chef, si cumencet a penser. AOI.
penser.tr: he bows his head, and begins to think. AOI.

penser.of: Li quens Rollant nel se doüst penser,
penser.tr: Count Roland ought not to have thought of it,

perdre.of: Asez est melz qu'il i perdent le chefs,
perdre.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads,

perdre.of: Que nus perduns l'onur ne la deintet,
perdre.tr: Than that we lose our honor and our dignity,

perdre.of: Asez est mielz, qu'il i perdent les testes,
perdre.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads there,

perdre.of: Que nus perduns clere Espaigne, la bele,
perdre.tr: Than that we lose bright Spain, the fair,

perdre.of: A ben petit que il ne pert le sens;
perdre.tr: he very nearly loses his mind;

perdre.of: Par jugement iloec perdrez le chef.
perdre.tr: By judgment there you will lose your head.

perdre.of: Dunc perdreit Carles le destre braz del cors,
perdre.tr: Then Charles would lose the right arm of his body,

perdre.of: N'i perdrat Carles, li reis ki France tient,
perdre.tr: Charles, the king who holds France, shall lose nothing by it,

perdre.of: Ne n'i perdrat ne runcin ne sumer,
perdre.tr: Nor shall he lose pack-horse or sumpter,

piller.tr: And had taken booty near Carcassonne;

plaire.tr: Ganelon answers this: «May it not please the Lord God!

plaire.of: Deus se lui plaist, a bien le vos mercie!» AOI.
plaire.tr: May God, if it please Him, reward you well for it!» AOI.

plaire.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Issi seit cum vos plaist!»
plaire.tr: Ganelon answered: «So be it, as it pleases you!»

pleurer.tr: There you would have seen so many knights weep,

pleurer.of: Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
pleurer.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

porter.of: Branches d'olives en voz mains porterez,
porter.tr: Olive branches you shall carry in your hands,

porter.of: Branches d'olive en voz mains portereiz,
porter.tr: Olive branches you shall carry in your hands,

porter.of: Enz en lur mains portent branches d'olive.
porter.tr: In their hands they bear branches of olive.
Each one carried an olive branch;

porter.of: Ne vos ne il n'i porterez les piez.
porter.tr: Neither you nor he shall set foot there.

porter.of: Qu'a Marsiliun me portast mun message.»
porter.tr: Who would carry my message to Marsile.»

porter.of: Tant vus avrai en curt a rei portee!
porter.tr: So long shall I have borne you at the king's court!

porter.of: Marsilies fait porter un livre avant:
porter.tr: Marsile has a book brought forward:

pouvoir.of: Nes poet guarder que mals ne l'i ateignet. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: he cannot keep harm from reaching him there. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Ben en purrat luer ses soldeiers.
pouvoir.tr: Well will he be able to pay his soldiers with it.

pouvoir.of: Dient paien: «Issi poet il ben estre!»
pouvoir.tr: The pagans say: «It may well be so!»

pouvoir.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
pouvoir.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

pouvoir.of: Nes poet guarder que alques ne l'engignent. AOI.
pouvoir.tr: He cannot guard against their deceiving him in some way. AOI.

pouvoir.of: Dunt bien purrez voz soldeiers luer.
pouvoir.tr: that you will well be able to pay your soldiers.

pouvoir.of: En quel mesure en purrai estre fiz?»
pouvoir.tr: In what measure can I be assured of it?»

pouvoir.of: Charles respunt: «Uncore purrat guarir.» AOI.
pouvoir.tr: Charles answers: «He may yet be saved.» AOI.

pouvoir.of: - «Seignurs baruns, qui i purruns enveier
pouvoir.tr: - «Lord barons, whom can we send

pouvoir.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
pouvoir.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

pouvoir.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
pouvoir.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

pouvoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Car il le poet ben faire;
pouvoir.tr: The Franks say: «For he can do it well;

pouvoir.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
pouvoir.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

pouvoir.of: - «Jo i puis aler, mais n'i avrai guarant: AOI.
pouvoir.tr: - «I can go there, but I shall have no protection: AOI.

pouvoir.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus! que purrat ço estre?
pouvoir.tr: The Franks say: «God! what can this be?

pouvoir.of: De ses meillors que il pout recuvrer:
pouvoir.tr: With the best of it that he could obtain:

pouvoir.of: Ne mul ne mule que puissez chevalcher;
pouvoir.tr: Nor mule nor she-mule that you may ride;

pouvoir.of: Sa grant valor, kil purreit acunter?
pouvoir.tr: His great valor, who could recount it?

pouvoir.of: Dist li paiens: «Mult me puis merveiller
pouvoir.tr: The pagan said: «Greatly may I marvel

pouvoir.of: Quarte cenz milie chevalers puis aveir.
pouvoir.tr: Four hundred thousand knights I can have.

pouvoir.of: Puis m'en cumbatre a Carlles e a Franceis?»
pouvoir.tr: Can I then fight against Charles and the Franks?»

pouvoir.of: «Cum faitement purrai Rollant ocire?»
pouvoir.tr: «How might I be able to kill Roland?»

pouvoir.of: - «Chi purreit faire, que Rollant i fust mort,
pouvoir.tr: - «Whoever could bring it about that Roland be killed there,

pouvoir.of: E, se il poet, murrat i veirement.
pouvoir.tr: And, if he can, he will truly die there.

pouvoir.of: Qu'en rereguarde trover le poüsum.»
pouvoir.tr: That we may find him in the rearguard.»

pouvoir.of: Par quel mesure le poüssum hunir.»
pouvoir.tr: As to by what means we might dishonor him.»

pouvoir.of: Sel pois trover a port ne a passage,
pouvoir.tr: If I can find him at pass or defile,

pouvoir.of: Ne poet muer que des oilz ne plurt.
pouvoir.tr: He cannot help but weep from his eyes.

prendre.of: Cordres ad prise e les murs peceiez,
prendre.tr: He has taken Cordres and shattered its walls,

prendre.of: Des ore cumencet le cunseill que mal prist. AOI.
prendre.tr: From now begins the counsel that came to ill. AOI.

prendre.of: Pris ai Valterne e la tere de Pine
prendre.tr: I have taken Valterne and the land of Pine

prendre.of: A vos Franceis un cunseill en presistes:
prendre.tr: With your Franks you took a counsel on it:

prendre.of: Les chef en prist es puis desuz Haltilie.
prendre.tr: He took their heads in the hills below Haltilie.

prendre.of: Quant le dut prendre, si li caït a tere.
prendre.tr: When he had to take it, it fell from him to the ground.

prendre.of: De guarnemenz se prent a cunreer,
prendre.tr: He sets about equipping himself with gear,

prendre.of: Pris e liez serez par poested;
prendre.tr: You will be seized and bound by force,

prendre.of: Par poestet serez pris e liez;
prendre.tr: By force you will be taken and bound;

prendre.of: Dunt pris les chefs as puis de Haltoíe;
prendre.tr: Whose heads I took at the heights of Haltoie;

prendre.of: E Guenes (l')ad pris par la main destre ad deiz,
prendre.tr: And Ganelon took him by the right hand by the fingers,

prendre.of: Tanz [colps] ad pris sur sun escut bucler,
prendre.tr: So many blows has he taken upon his buckler-shield,

prendre.of: Tanz colz ad pris de lances e d'espiet,
prendre.tr: so many blows he has taken from lances and spears,

prendre.of: Tanz colps ad pris de bons espiez trenchanz,
prendre.tr: So many blows has he taken from good sharp spears,

prendre.of: Il les ad prises, en sa hoese les butet, AOI.
prendre.tr: He took them, into his boot he stuffed them, AOI.

prendre.of: Li quens Rollant il l'ad e prise e fraite;
prendre.tr: Count Roland had taken and shattered it;

prendre.of: Les castels pris, (. . .) les citez violees.
prendre.tr: taken the castles, (. . .) violated the cities.

prier.of: Tuit li preierent li meillor Sarrazin,
prier.tr: All the noblest Saracens begged him,

priser.of: Tant nel vos sai ne preiser ne loer
priser.tr: I cannot so praise him to you nor extol him

protéger.of: Par Charlemagne n'er(cs) guariz ne tensez.
protéger.tr: Will not be protected nor defended by Charlemagne.

présenter.of: «De trestuz reis vos present les curunes.»
présenter.tr: «Of all kings I present to you the crowns.»

présenter.of: Le grant aveir en presentez al rei Carles,
présenter.tr: Present its great wealth to King Charles,

quereller.of: Jo me crendreie, que vos vos meslisez.
quereller.tr: I would fear that you would come to blows.

raconter.of: Sa grant valor, kil purreit acunter?
raconter.tr: His great valor, who could recount it?

recevoir.of: Si recevrez la lei de chrestiens,
recevoir.tr: Thus you will receive the law of the Christians,
And I will receive the Christian faith,

recevoir.of: Si recevrat la nostre lei plus salve;
recevoir.tr: And there will receive our most saving faith;

recevoir.of: Puis recevrat la lei que nus tenum,
recevoir.tr: Then will receive the faith that we hold,

recevoir.of: Si recevez le bastun e lu guant.
recevoir.tr: And receive the staff and the glove.

recevoir.of: Getet le a tere, sil receit Blancandrin;
recevoir.tr: He throws it to the ground, and Blancandrin catches it;

recevoir.of: Que recevez la lei de chrestiens;
recevoir.tr: That you receive the faith of Christians;

recevoir.of: Si recevrat la lei que vos tenez,
recevoir.tr: And he will receive the faith that you hold,

recevoir.of: De sa main destre, quant reçut le bastun.»
recevoir.tr: from his right hand, when he received the staff.»

recouvrer.of: De ses meillors que il pout recuvrer:
recouvrer.tr: With the best of it that he could obtain:

redresser.of: Quant se redrecet, mult par out fier lu vis;
redresser.tr: When he straightens up, his face looked most proud;

refuser.tr: Ganelon answers: «I do not refuse it at all.

regarder.of: Tant par fut bels tuit si per l'en esguardent.
regarder.tr: He was so very handsome that all his peers gaze at him.

regarder.of: Guardet al bref, vit la raisun escrite:
regarder.tr: He looks at the letter, saw the writing set down there:

regarder.of: Par mi cel host suvent e menu reguarded:.
regarder.tr: Throughout that host he looks about frequently and closely.

regarder.of: Quant l'ot li reis, fierement le reguardet,
regarder.tr: When the king heard this, fiercely he looks at him,

rejeter.of: Ki ço vos lodet que cest plait degetuns,
rejeter.tr: Whoever advises you that we reject this agreement,

remembrer.of: Que me remembre de la dolur e (de) l'ire,
remembrer.tr: That I remember the grief and the wrath,

rendre.of: Une bataille lur i rendent cil primes;
rendre.tr: Let these give them a first battle there;

rentrer.of: En France, ad Ais, s'en deit ben repairer.
rentrer.tr: To France, to Aix, he ought well to return.

rentrer.of: En France, ad Ais, devez bien repairer;
rentrer.tr: to France, to Aix, you ought rightly to return;

rentrer.of: Se Deus ço dunet que jo de la repaire,
rentrer.tr: If God grants that I return from there,

rentrer.of: Hom ki la vait, repairer ne s'en poet.
rentrer.tr: A man who goes there cannot return from it.

rentrer.of: En dulce France s'en repairerat li reis;
rentrer.tr: The king will return to sweet France;

reprocher.tr: To my knowledge they will not reproach me

requérir.of: Que nus requert ça en la nostre marche?»
requérir.tr: Why does he make demands on us here in our march?»

rester.of: N'i ad castel ki devant lui remaigne;
rester.tr: There is no fortress that stands against him;

rester.of: Mur ne citet n'i est remés a fraindre
rester.tr: no wall or city is left to be breached

rester.of: En la citet nen ad remes paien,
rester.tr: In the city there remains no pagan,

rester.of: E dist al rei: «Laisez ester voz Francs!
rester.tr: And said to the king: «Let your Franks be!

rester.of: E est remes en sun blialt de palie.
rester.tr: And he remained in his tunic of silk.
And the marvelous armies would be left behind;
The Great Land would remain in peace.»

rester.of: En un bruill par sum les puis remestrent,
rester.tr: In a wood atop the heights they halted,

retentir.of: N'i ad celoi ki mot sunt ne mot tint,
retentir.tr: There is none among them who speaks a word nor utters a word,

revêtir.tr: He is wrapped in a sable mantle,

rire.of: Quant l'ot Rollant, si cumençat a rire. AOI.
rire.tr: When Roland heard it, he began to laugh. AOI.

rire.of: Quant ço veit Guenes que ore s'en rit Rollant,
rire.tr: When Ganelon sees that Roland now laughs about it,

rire.of: Cler en riant l'ad dit a Guenelun:
rire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

rire.of: Cler en riant a Guenelun l'ad dit:
rire.tr: Brightly, laughing, he said to Ganelon:

rompre.of: Ne n'ai tel gent ki la sue derumpet.
rompre.tr: Nor have I such a host as might break his.

réclamer.tr: serves Mahomet and calls upon Apollin;

réfléchir.of: Mais li quens Guenes se fut ben purpenset.
réfléchir.tr: But Count Ganelon had thought it over well.

répondre.of: N'i ad paien ki un sul mot respundet,
répondre.tr: There is no pagan who answers a single word,

répondre.of: Charles respunt: «Uncore purrat guarir.» AOI.
répondre.tr: Charles answers: «He may yet be saved.» AOI.

répondre.of: Ne ben ne mal ne respunt sun nevuld.
répondre.tr: Neither good nor ill does he answer his nephew.

répondre.of: Guenes li quens ço vus ad respondud,
répondre.tr: Ganelon the count has answered you thus,

répondre.of: Respunt dux Neimes: «Jo irai par vostre dun!
répondre.tr: Duke Naimes replies: «I will go, by your leave!

répondre.of: Respunt li reis: «Vos estes saives hom;
répondre.tr: The king replies: «You are a wise man;

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Jo i puis aler mult ben!»
répondre.tr: Roland answers: «I can go there very well!»

répondre.of: Respunt li reis: «Ambdui vos en taisez!
répondre.tr: The king answers: «Both of you, be silent about it!

répondre.of: Li empereres respunt par maltalant:
répondre.tr: The emperor answers with displeasure:

répondre.of: Respunt Rollant: «Orgoill ói e folage.
répondre.tr: Rollant answers: «I hear arrogance and folly.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Pur mei n'iras tu mie!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «You will not go at all on my behalf!

répondre.of: Carles respunt: «trop avez tendre coer.
répondre.tr: Charles answers: «you have too tender a heart.

répondre.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Ne placet Damnedeu!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers this: «May it not please the Lord God!

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Itels est sis curages,
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Such is his temper,

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo ne sai veirs nul hume,
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «I know of no man truly,

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Par la franceise gent.
répondre.tr: Ganelon replies: «With the French people.

répondre.of: Respunt Marsilie: «Or diet, nus l'orrum!» AOI.
répondre.tr: Marsile replies: «Let him speak now, we shall hear him!» AOI.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo nel desotrei mie.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «I do not refuse it at all.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Carles n'est mie tels.
répondre.tr: Ganelon replies: «Charles is not at all such a man.

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Ne vus a ceste feiz!
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Not this time!

répondre.of: Guenes respont: «Ço vos sai jo ben dire.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «That I can well tell you.

répondre.of: Ço respunt Guenes: «Issi seit cum vos plaist!»
répondre.tr: Ganelon answered: «So be it, as it pleases you!»

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Ben seit vostre comant!» AOI.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «May your command be a good one!» AOI.

répondre.of: - «Ben serat fait,» li quens Guenes respunt.
répondre.tr: - «It will be well done,» answers Count Ganelon.

répondre.of: - «Ben serat fait,» Guenes respundit.
répondre.tr: - «It will be well done,» Ganelon answered.

répondre.of: E cil respunt: «Oïl, sire, asez bien:
répondre.tr: And he replies: «Yes, lord, very amply:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Mei est vis que trop targe!»
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «It seems to me that I tarry too long!»

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Rollant cist miens fillastre:
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Roland, this stepson of mine:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Oger de Denemarche:
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «Ogier of Denmark:

répondre.of: Guenes respunt: «Veir dites, jol sai bien.» AOI.
répondre.tr: Ganelon answers: «You speak truly, I know it well.» AOI.

saisir.of: Sis aquillit e tempeste e ored:
saisir.tr: A storm and a tempest seized them:

saisir.of: Guenes li quens l'ad sur lui saisie;
saisir.tr: Ganelon the count has seized it from him;

saluer.of: Sil saluerent par amur e par bien.
saluer.tr: And greeted him with love and good will.

saluer.of: De meie part ma muiller saluez,
saluer.tr: On my behalf greet my wife,

savoir.of: Par voz saveirs sem puez acorder,
savoir.tr: By your wisdom, if you can bring me to an accord,

savoir.of: Mais jo ne sai quels en est sis curages.»
savoir.tr: But I do not know what his intention is.»

savoir.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
savoir.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

savoir.of: Ço set hom ben que jo sui tis parastres;
savoir.tr: One knows well that I am your stepfather;

savoir.of: Ço set hom ben, n'ai cure de manace;
savoir.tr: One knows well, I have no care for a threat;
«I know well that I must go to Saragossa.

savoir.of: E Baldewin, mun filz que vos savez,
savoir.tr: And Baldwin, my son whom you know,

savoir.of: Guenes respunt: «Jo ne sai veirs nul hume,
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «I know of no man truly,

savoir.of: Par grant saver cumencet a parler
savoir.tr: With great wisdom he begins to speak,

savoir.of: Cume celui ki ben faire le set,
savoir.tr: Like one who well knows how to do it,

savoir.of: Ço dist Marsilies: «Guenes par veir sacez,
savoir.tr: Marsile said this: «Ganelon, know in truth,

savoir.of: N'est hom kil veit e conuistre le set
savoir.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

savoir.of: Tant nel vos sai ne preiser ne loer
savoir.tr: I cannot so praise him to you nor extol him

savoir.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
savoir.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.

savoir.of: Guenes respont: «Ço vos sai jo ben dire.
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «That I can well tell you.

savoir.of: Cunseill n'est proz dunt hume n'est sevus.
savoir.tr: Counsel is not worthwhile of which no man can be sure.

savoir.of: Deus! quel dulur que li Franceis nel sevent! AOI.
savoir.tr: God! what grief that the French do not know it! AOI.

savoir.of: Il ne sevent, liquels d'els la veintrat.
savoir.tr: They do not know which of the two will win it.

savoir.of: Guenes respunt: «Veir dites, jol sai bien.» AOI.
savoir.tr: Ganelon answers: «You speak truly, I know it well.» AOI.

secouer.of: De sun algeir ad la hanste crollee.
secouer.tr: With his javelin he has shaken the shaft.

servir.tr: serves Mahomet and calls upon Apollin;

songer.of: Sunjat qu'il eret al greignurs porz de Sizer,
songer.tr: He dreamt that he was at the great passes of Cize,
After this one he dreamed another vision:

sonner.of: Par mi cel ost funt mil grailles suner;
sonner.tr: Throughout that host they have a thousand trumpets sounded;

souffrir.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «it falls to me to endure it;

soulager.of: Que jo n'esclair ceste meie grant ire.»
soulager.tr: To relieve this great wrath of mine.»

suivre.of: Vos le sivrez a la feste seint Michel:
suivre.tr: You will follow him there at the feast of Saint Michael:

suivre.of: Que jel sivrai od mil de mes fedeilz,
suivre.tr: Before I follow him with a thousand of my faithful,

suivre.of: La vos sivrat, ço dit mis avoez.»
suivre.tr: there he will follow you, so says my lord.»

suivre.of: Mis avoez la vos sivrat, ço dit;
suivre.tr: My lord will follow you there, so he says;

suivre.of: Il me sivrat ad Aís, a mun estage,
suivre.tr: He will follow me to Aix, to my residence,

suivre.of: Qu'il vos sivrat en France le regnet,
suivre.tr: Before he will follow you into France the realm,

susciter.of: Jo t'en muvra[i] un si grant contr[a]ire
susciter.tr: I will stir up against you such a great calamity

taire.of: Franceis se taisent ne mais que Guenelun,
taire.tr: The French keep silent, all save Ganelon,

taire.of: Respunt li reis: «Ambdui vos en taisez!
taire.tr: The king answers: «Both of you, be silent about it!

taire.of: Franceis se taisent: as les vus aquisez.
taire.tr: The Franks fall silent: behold them quieted.

tarder.of: Quant aler dei, n'i ai plus que targer.»
tarder.tr: Since I must depart, there is no more reason for me to tarry.»

tarder.of: Guenes respunt: «Mei est vis que trop targe!»
tarder.tr: Ganelon answers: «It seems to me that I tarry too long!»

tendre.of: Li empereres tent (...) ses mains vers Deu,
tendre.tr: The emperor stretches out his hands toward God,

tendre.of: El grant verger fait li reis tendre un tref,
tendre.tr: In the great orchard the king has a tent pitched,

tendre.of: Li empereres li tent sun guant le destre;
tendre.tr: The emperor holds out to him his right glove;

tenir.of: Li reis Marsilie la tient, ki Deu nen amet,
tenir.tr: King Marsile holds it — he who does not love God,

tenir.of: A seint Michel tendrat mult halte feste.
tenir.tr: At Saint Michael's he will hold a very high feast.

tenir.of: La siet li reis, ki dulce france tient.
tenir.tr: There sits the king who holds sweet France.

tenir.of: Li empereres en tint sun chef enclin;
tenir.tr: The emperor kept his head bowed;

tenir.of: Chrestiens ert, de mei tendrat ses marches;
tenir.tr: He will be a Christian, will hold his marches from me;

tenir.of: Li empe[re]re en tint sun chef enbrunc,
tenir.tr: The emperor held his head bowed down,

tenir.of: E tute Espaigne tendrat par vostre dun,
tenir.tr: And will hold all Spain by your gift,

tenir.of: Puis recevrat la lei que nus tenum,
tenir.tr: Then will receive the faith that we hold,

tenir.of: Laissun les fols, as sages nus tenuns.» AOI.
tenir.tr: Let us leave the fools, let us hold to the wise.» AOI.

tenir.of: Al Sarrazin ki Sarraguce tient?»
tenir.tr: To the Saracen who holds Saragossa?»

tenir.of: L'estreu li tint sun uncle Guinemer.
tenir.tr: His uncle Guinemer held the stirrup for him.

tenir.of: E lui aidez e pur seignur le tenez.»
tenir.tr: And help him and hold him as your lord.»

tenir.of: En sa main tint une vermeille pume:
tenir.tr: In his hand he held a crimson apple:

tenir.of: «Tenez bel sire,» dist Rollant a sun uncle,
tenir.tr: «Take it, fair lord,» said Roland to his uncle,

tenir.of: La fut li reis ki tute Espaigne tint;
tenir.tr: There was the king who held all Spain;

tenir.of: Par le puig[n] tint le cunte Guenelun,
tenir.tr: By the fist he held the count Ganelon,

tenir.of: E d'Apollin, qui seintes leis tenuns!
tenir.tr: And by Apollo, whose holy laws we hold!

tenir.of: Un algier tint, ki d'or fut enpenet,
tenir.tr: He held a javelin, which was feathered with gold,

tenir.of: En sun puign destre par l'orie punt la tint.
tenir.tr: In his right fist he held it by the golden pommel.

tenir.of: Quar ço vos mandet Carles, ki France tient,
tenir.tr: For thus Charles, who rules France, commands you:

tenir.of: Lessez (la) folie, tenez vos al saveir.
tenir.tr: Leave aside folly, hold to wisdom.

tenir.of: «Tenez m'espee, meillur n'en at nuls hom;
tenir.tr: «Take my sword, no man has a better one;

tenir.of: «Tenez mun helme, unches meillor ne vi.
tenir.tr: «Take my helmet, never did I see a better one.

tenir.of: Marsilies tint Guen[elun] par l'espalle;
tenir.tr: Marsile took Ganelon by the shoulder;

tenir.of: Par cele lei que vos tenez plus salve,
tenir.tr: By the law that you hold most sacred,

tenir.of: Tenez les clefs de ceste citet large,
tenir.tr: Take the keys of this great city,

tenir.of: Que il ne voelent ne tenir ne guarder.
tenir.tr: Which they wish neither to hold nor to keep.

tenir.of: Si recevrat la lei que vos tenez,
tenir.tr: And he will receive the faith that you hold,

tenir.of: De vos tendrat Espaigne le regnet.»
tenir.tr: From you he will hold Spain the realm.»

tenir.of: Entre ses poinz teneit sa hanste fraisnine.
tenir.tr: Between his hands he held his ashen lance.

tenir.of: N'i perdrat Carles, li reis ki France tient,
tenir.tr: Charles, the king who holds France, shall lose nothing by it,

tenir.of: «Dunez mei l'arc, que vos tenez el poign.
tenir.tr: «Give me the bow that you hold in your fist.

tenir.of: Li empereres en tint sun chef enbrunc,
tenir.tr: The emperor held his head bowed,

tomber.of: Quant le dut prendre, si li caït a tere.
tomber.tr: When he had to take it, it fell from him to the ground.

tomber.of: Carlles verrat sun grant orguill cadeir;
tomber.tr: Charles will see his great pride fall;

tomber.of: Qui[d]ás, le guant me caïst en la place,
tomber.tr: Did you think the glove would fall to me on the spot,

tomber.of: Que il me chedet, cum fist a Guenelun
tomber.tr: that it should fall from me, as it happened to Ganelon

tordre.of: Si duist sa barbe, e detoerst sun gernun;
tordre.tr: He smoothed his beard, and twisted his moustache;

tourner.of: Sur mei avez turnet fals jugement.
tourner.tr: you have turned a false judgment upon me.

tourner.of: Guardez de nos ne turnez le curage.
tourner.tr: Take care that you do not turn your heart away from us.

traiter.tr: The caliph said: «You have served us ill,

trancher.of: De noz ostages ferat tre[n]cher les testes;
trancher.tr: He will have the heads of our hostages cut off;

trancher.of: Tanz colps ad pris de bons espiez trenchanz,
trancher.tr: So many blows has he taken from good sharp spears,

trancher.of: La destre oreille al premer uer trenchat,
trancher.tr: It tore off the right ear of the first beast,

transmettre.of: Que li tramist li reis de Suatilie;
transmettre.tr: Which the king of Suatilie had sent to him;

transmettre.of: «Li reis Marsilie m'ad tramis ses messages;
transmettre.tr: «King Marsile has sent me his messengers;

transmettre.of: Dous de voz cuntes al paien tramesistes,
transmettre.tr: Two of your counts you sent to the pagan,

transmettre.of: Se lui lessez, n'i trametrez plus saive.»
transmettre.tr: If you leave him aside, you will not send a wiser one.»

travailler.of: Lui e altrui travaillent, e cunfundent.»
travailler.tr: They torment him and others, and bring ruin.»

travailler.of: Par tantes teres ad sun cors traveillet,
travailler.tr: Through so many lands he has worn out his body,

tromper.of: Nes poet guarder que alques ne l'engignent. AOI.
tromper.tr: He cannot guard against their deceiving him in some way. AOI.

trousser.of: D'or e d'argent .IIII. cenz muls trussez,
trousser.tr: four hundred mules loaded with gold and silver,

trousser.of: Franc desherbergent, funt lur sumers trosser:
trousser.tr: The Franks break camp, have their pack-horses loaded:

trouver.of: Se en rereguarde troevet le cors Rollant,
trouver.tr: If in the rearguard he finds Roland's person,
That we may find him in the rearguard.»

trouver.of: Sel pois trover a port ne a passage,
trouver.tr: If I can find him at pass or defile,

tuer.of: Par nun d'ocire i enveierai le men.
tuer.tr: Even at the risk of death I will send mine.

tuer.of: Ne seit ocis, u devient chrestien.
tuer.tr: Who is not slain, or becomes a Christian.

tuer.of: Pa[r] num de ocire i metrai un mien filz,
tuer.tr: At risk of being slain, I will put there a son of mine,

tuer.of: Si vengez cels que li fels fist ocire!» AOI.
tuer.tr: And avenge those whom the villain had killed!» AOI.

tuer.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
tuer.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

tuer.of: Que il querreient, que Rollant fust ocis.
tuer.tr: That they would seek a way for Roland to be killed.
«How might I be able to kill Roland?»

user.of: Il est mult vielz, si ad sun tens uset;
user.tr: He is very old, and has worn out his time;

vaincre.of: Li reis Marsilie est de guere vencud:
vaincre.tr: King Marsile is defeated in war:

vaincre.of: Ses citez arses e ses humes vencuz;
vaincre.tr: Burned his cities and vanquished his men;

vaincre.of: Tanz riches reis morz e vencuz en champ:
vaincre.tr: So many rich kings dead and vanquished in the field:

vaincre.of: Il ne sevent, liquels d'els la veintrat.
vaincre.tr: They do not know which of the two will win it.

valoir.of: Jamais n'ert hume ki encuntre lui vaille.» AOI.
valoir.tr: Never will there be a man who can match himself against him.» AOI.

valoir.of: Melz en valt l'or que ne funt cinc cenz livres:
valoir.tr: their gold is worth more than five hundred pounds:

valoir.of: Eles valent mielz que tut l'aveir de Rume,
valoir.tr: They are worth more than all the wealth of Rome,

venger.of: Si vengez cels que li fels fist ocire!» AOI.
venger.tr: And avenge those whom the villain had killed!» AOI.

venir.of: En cest païs nos est venuz cunfundre.
venir.tr: Has come into this land to destroy us.

venir.of: Vendrat li jurz, si passerat li termes,
venir.tr: The day will come, and so the term will pass,

venir.of: Vindrent a Charles ki France ad en baillie:
venir.tr: They came to Charles, who has France in his keeping:

venir.of: La noit demurent tresque vint al jur cler.
venir.tr: The night they remain until the bright day came.

venir.of: Ensembl' od els li quens Rollant i vint,
venir.tr: Together with them Count Roland came there,

venir.of: Guenes i vint, ki la traïsun fist.
venir.tr: Guenes came there, who committed the treason.

venir.of: En piez se drecet, si li vint cuntredire.
venir.tr: Rises to his feet, and came to contradict him.

venir.of: Set anz [ad] pleins, que en Espaigne venimes;
venir.tr: Seven full years it is, since we came into Spain;

venir.of: En piez se drecet, si vint devant Carlun,
venir.tr: He rises to his feet, and came before Charles,

venir.of: Apres iço i est Neimes venud;
venir.tr: After this, Naimes came forward;

venir.of: Ço dist li reis: «Guenes venez avant. AOI.
venir.tr: The king said: «Ganelon, come forward. AOI.

venir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
venir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

venir.of: Blancandrins vint devant l'empereür;
venir.tr: Blancandrin came before the emperor;

venir.of: En Sarraguce vus vendrat aseger;
venir.tr: He will come to besiege you in Saragossa;

venir.of: E Blancandrins i vint, al canud peil,
venir.tr: And Blancandrin came there, with the hoary hair,

venir.of: Puis si cumencet a venir ses tresors. AOI.
venir.tr: Then he begins to bring forth his treasures. AOI.

venir.of: A tant i vint uns paiens, Valdabruns:
venir.tr: Thereupon came a pagan, Valdabrun:

venir.of: Apres i vint un paien, Climorins.
venir.tr: After him came a pagan, Climorins.

venir.of: A tant i vint la reine Bramimunde.
venir.tr: Then came the queen Bramimonde.

venir.of: Venuz en est a la citet de Galne.
venir.tr: He has come to the city of Galne.

venir.of: Guenes li quens est venuz as herberges. AOI.
venir.tr: Ganelon the count has come to the encampment. AOI.

venir.of: Guenes i vint, li fels, li parjurez.
venir.tr: Ganelon came there, the felon, the perjurer.

venir.of: Devers Ardene vit venir uns leuparz,
venir.tr: From toward the Ardennes he saw a leopard coming,

venir.of: Que vint a Carles le galops e les salz,
venir.tr: Which came to Charles at a gallop and by leaps,

violer.of: Les castels pris, (. . .) les citez violees.
violer.tr: taken the castles, (. . .) violated the cities.

vivre.of: Ne l'amerai a trestut mun vivant,
vivre.tr: I shall not love him all the days of my life,

vivre.of: Tant ad erret nen est dreiz que plus vivet.
vivre.tr: He has gone so far that it is not right that he live longer.

vivre.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes: «tant cum vivet sis niés:
vivre.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «as long as his nephew lives:

vivre.of: - «Ço n'iert,» dist Guenes, «tant cum vivet Rollant:
vivre.tr: - «That will not be,» said Ganelon, «so long as Roland lives:

vivre.of: Soürs est Carlles, ne (cre) crent hume vivant.» AOI.
vivre.tr: Charles is secure, he fears no living man.» AOI.

voir.of: L'ost des Franceis verrez sempres desfere.
voir.tr: You will soon see the host of the Franks disband.

voir.of: Ja einz ne verrat passer cest premer meis,
voir.tr: He will not see this first month pass
By this beard that you see growing white,

voir.of: Sin vois vedeir alques de sun semblant.»
voir.tr: And go see something of his disposition.»

voir.tr: Rightful emperor, here you see me present,

There you would have seen so many knights weep,

voir.of: Quant le vit Guenes, mist la main a l'espee,

voir.tr: There is no man who sees him and comes to know him

voir.tr: «I have such troops, you will never see finer;

voir.tr: Charles will see his great pride fall;

voir.of: «Tenez mun helme, unches meillor ne vi.
voir.tr: «Take my helmet, never did I see a better one.

voir.tr: For with my own eyes I saw four hundred thousand armed men,

voir.of: La sunt neiez, jamais nes en verrez;

voir.of: Ja ne verrez cest premer meis passet
voir.tr: Never will you see this first month pass

voir.tr: From toward the Ardennes he saw a leopard coming,

voir.of: «Veez les porz e les destreiz passages:
voir.tr: «You see the passes and the narrow defiles:

voler.of: Qu'envers le cel en volent les escicles.
voler.tr: That toward the sky the splinters fly.

vouloir.of: S'il voelt ostages, il en avrat par veir.»
vouloir.tr: If he wants hostages, he shall have them indeed.»

vouloir.tr: of his wealth he wishes to give you plenty,

vouloir.of: - «Voet par hostages,» ço dist li Sarrazins,
vouloir.tr: - «By hostages,» said the Saracen,

vouloir.of: La vuldrat il chrestiens devenir.»
vouloir.tr: There he will wish to become a Christian.»

vouloir.tr: Through those of France he wishes to act in everything. AOI.

vouloir.of: De sun aveir me voelt duner grant masse,
vouloir.tr: From his wealth he wishes to give me a great quantity,

vouloir.of: U par ostage vos (en) voelt faire soürs;
vouloir.tr: Or by hostages he wishes to give you assurance;

vouloir.of: Se li reis voelt, jo i puis aler ben.»
vouloir.tr: If the king wills it, I can well go there.»

vouloir.of: Si li reis voelt, prez sui por vus le face.» AOI.
vouloir.tr: If the king wishes, I am ready to do it for you.» AOI.

vouloir.of: Ademplir voeill vostre comandement.» AOI.
vouloir.tr: I will fulfill your command.» AOI.

vouloir.of: Mais li quens Guenes iloec ne volsist estre:
vouloir.tr: But Count Ganelon would not wish to be there:

vouloir.of: Ki tute gent voelt faire recreant,
vouloir.tr: He who wants to make every people cry for mercy,

vouloir.of: Pur les nuveles qu'il vuldreient oïr.
vouloir.tr: Because of the news they would wish to hear.

vouloir.of: Demi Espaigne vos voelt en fiu duner.
vouloir.tr: He wishes to give you half of Spain in fief.

vouloir.of: Se cest acorde ne vulez otrier,
vouloir.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

vouloir.of: Ferir l'en volt, se n'en fust desturnet. AOI.
vouloir.tr: He wished to strike him with it, had he not been prevented. AOI.

vouloir.of: Mais de s'espee ne volt mie guerpir;
vouloir.tr: But he would by no means give up his sword;

vouloir.of: Si ceste acorde ne volez otrier,
vouloir.tr: If you will not grant this accord,

vouloir.of: Se de mun cors voeil aquiter la vie,
vouloir.tr: If I wish to ransom the life of my body,

vouloir.of: En talant ai que mult vos voeill amer,
vouloir.tr: I have the desire that I much want to love you,

vouloir.of: De Carlemagne vos voeill oïr parler.
vouloir.tr: I want to hear you speak of Charlemagne.

vouloir.of: Meilz voelt murir que guerpir sun barnet.»
vouloir.tr: He would rather die than abandon his noble company.»

vouloir.of: De mun aveir vos voeill dunner grant masse:
vouloir.tr: Of my wealth I will give you a great quantity:

vouloir.of: Que il ne voelent ne tenir ne guarder.
vouloir.tr: Which they wish neither to hold nor to keep.

voyager.of: Par cels de France voelt il del tut errer. AOI.
voyager.tr: Through those of France he wishes to act in everything. AOI.

vêtir.of: Vint i ses nies, out vestue sa brunie,
vêtir.tr: His nephew came, had donned his hauberk,

vêtir.of: Halbercs vestuz e tres bien fermeez
vêtir.tr: hauberks donned and very well fastened,

échapper.of: De quel que seit Rollant n'estuertrat mie.
échapper.tr: From one or the other Roland will not escape.

échoir.tr: - «Sire,» said Ganelon, «it falls to me to endure it;

éclairer.of: Par main en l'albe, si cum li jurz esclairet,
éclairer.tr: At morning, in the dawn, as the day grows bright,

écouter.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
écouter.tr: Mass and matins the king has heard.

écouter.of: Vos le doüssez esculter e oïr.»
écouter.tr: You ought to have listened to him and heard him out.»

écouter.of: Messe e matines ad li reis escultet.
écouter.tr: Mass and matins has the king heard.

écrire.of: Guardet al bref, vit la raisun escrite:
écrire.tr: He looks at the letter, saw the writing set down there:

équiper.tr: Twelve sergeants have well equipped them.

équiper.of: De guarnemenz se prent a cunreer,
équiper.tr: He sets about equipping himself with gear,

équiper.of: Escuz as cols e lances adubees.
équiper.tr: shields at their necks and lances equipped.

étonner.of: Dist li paiens: «Mult me puis merveiller
étonner.tr: The pagan said: «Greatly may I marvel

étonner.of: N'i ait Franceis ki tot ne s'en merveilt.
étonner.tr: That there will be no Frank who does not marvel at it.

éveiller.of: Carles se dort, qu'il ne s'esveillet mie.
éveiller.tr: Charles sleeps, for he does not wake at all.

éveiller.of: Carles se dort, mie ne s'esveillat. AOI.
éveiller.tr: Charles sleeps on, he did not wake at all. AOI.

être.of: set anz tuz pleins ad estet en Espaigne:
être.tr: has been in Spain seven full years:

être.of: Mur ne citet n'i est remés a fraindre
être.tr: no wall or city is left to be breached

être.of: Fors Saraguce, ki est en une muntaigne.
être.tr: except Saragossa, which sits upon a mountain.

être.of: Li reis Marsilie esteit en Sarraguce.
être.tr: King Marsile was in Saragossa.

être.of: Alez en est en un verger suz l'umbre;
être.tr: He has gone off into an orchard beneath the shade;

être.of: En cest païs nos est venuz cunfundre.
être.tr: Has come into this land to destroy us.

être.of: Blancandrins fut des plus saives paiens:
être.tr: Blancandrin was among the wisest pagans:

être.of: De vasselage fut asez chevaler,
être.tr: In valor he was very much a knight,

être.of: Serez ses hom par honur e par ben.
être.tr: You will be his man in honor and in good faith.

être.of: Asez est melz qu'il i perdent le chefs,
être.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads,

être.of: Ne nus seiuns cunduiz a mendeier.» AOI.
être.tr: And that we be reduced to begging.» AOI.

être.of: Quant cascuns ert a sun meillor repaire,
être.tr: When each is back at his best dwelling,

être.of: Carles serat ad Ais, a sa capele;
être.tr: Charles will be at Aix, in his chapel;

être.of: Li reis est fiers e sis curages pesmes:
être.tr: The king is fierce and his heart is cruel:

être.of: Asez est mielz, qu'il i perdent les testes,
être.tr: It is far better that they lose their heads there,

être.of: Dient paien: «Issi poet il ben estre!»
être.tr: The pagans say: «It may well be so!»

être.of: Il est al siege a Cordres la citet.
être.tr: He is at the siege of the city of Cordres.

être.of: [S]erai ses hom par amur e par feid;
être.tr: I will be his man through love and through faith;

être.of: Li frein sunt d'or, les seles d'argent mises.
être.tr: The bridles are of gold, the saddles set with silver.

être.of: Cil sunt muntez ki le message firent,
être.tr: Those who carried the message have mounted,

être.of: Ne seit ocis, u devient chrestien.
être.tr: Who is not slain, or becomes a Christian.

être.of: Li empereres est en un grant verger,
être.tr: The emperor is in a great orchard,

être.of: E si i furent e gerin et gerers,
être.tr: And there too were Gerin and Gerier,

être.of: La u cist furent, des altres i out bien:
être.tr: Where these were, there were indeed many others:

être.of: S'est, kil demandet, ne l estoet enseigner.
être.tr: He is so, whoever seeks him, that there is no need to point him out.

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
être.tr: and said to the king: «Be saved by God

être.of: En cest païs avez estet asez;
être.tr: In this land you have stayed long enough;

être.of: De sa parole ne fut mie hastifs:
être.tr: He was in no way hasty with his speech:

être.of: Sa custume est qu'il parolet a leisír.
être.tr: His custom is that he speaks at leisure.

être.of: Li reis Marsilies est mult mis enemis.
être.tr: King Marsile is a great enemy of mine.

être.of: En quel mesure en purrai estre fiz?»
être.tr: In what measure can I be assured of it?»

être.of: Quant vus serez el palais seignurill,
être.tr: When you are in the lordly palace,

être.of: Bels fut li vespres e li soleilz fut cler.
être.tr: Beautiful was the evening and the sun was bright.

être.of: Li empereres est par matin levet;
être.tr: The emperor is risen at morning;

être.of: Desuz un pin en est li reis alez,
être.tr: Beneath a pine the king has gone,

être.of: E si i furent e Gerers e Gerin;
être.tr: And there too were Gerers and Gerin;

être.of: Chrestiens ert, de mei tendrat ses marches;
être.tr: He will be a Christian, will hold his marches from me;

être.of: Mais jo ne sai quels en est sis curages.»
être.tr: But I do not know what his intention is.»

être.of: L'un fut Basan e li altres Basilies;
être.tr: The one was Basan and the other Basile;

être.of: Apres iço i est Neimes venud;
être.tr: After this, Naimes came forward;

être.of: Saveir i ad, mais qu'il seit entendud.
être.tr: There is wisdom in it, provided it be heeded.

être.of: Li reis Marsilie est de guere vencud:
être.tr: King Marsile is defeated in war:

être.of: Respunt li reis: «Vos estes saives hom;
être.tr: The king replies: «You are a wise man;

être.of: «Vostre curages est mult pesmes e fiers;
être.tr: «Your temper is very harsh and fierce;

être.of: Li duze per mar i serunt jugez!»
être.tr: The twelve peers would be ill-judged [for it]!»

être.of: Turpins de Reins en est levet del renc,
être.tr: Turpin of Reims has risen from the ranks,

être.of: En cest païs avez estet set anz;
être.tr: In this land you have been seven years;

être.of: Ço dist Rollant: «Ço ert Guenes, mis parastre.»
être.tr: Rollant said this: «That will be Guenes, my stepfather.»

être.of: E li quens Guenes en fut mult anguisables;
être.tr: And the count Guenes was very distressed by it;

être.of: E est remes en sun blialt de palie.
être.tr: And he remained in his tunic of silk.

être.of: Tant par fut bels tuit si per l'en esguardent.
être.tr: He was so very handsome that all his peers gaze at him.

être.of: Ço set hom ben que jo sui tis parastres;
être.tr: One knows well that I am your stepfather;

être.of: Si li reis voelt, prez sui por vus le face.» AOI.
être.tr: If the king wishes, I am ready to do it for you.» AOI.

être.of: Tu n'ies mes hom ne jo ne sui tis sire.
être.tr: You are not my vassal, nor am I your lord.

être.of: Ço est Baldewin,» ço dit, «ki ert prozdoem.
être.tr: it is Baldwin,» he says, «who will be a worthy man.

être.of: Ne Oliver, por ço qu'il est si cumpainz;
être.tr: Nor Oliver, because he is his companion;

être.of: Mais li quens Guenes iloec ne volsist estre:
être.tr: But Count Ganelon would not wish to be there:

être.of: Dient Franceis: «Deus! que purrat ço estre?
être.tr: The Franks say: «God! what can this be?

être.of: Ki tuit li dient «Tant mare fustes, ber!
être.tr: Who all say to him: «What an evil hour was yours, baron!

être.of: En (la) cort al rei mult i avez ested,
être.tr: In the king's court you have long been,

être.of: Par Charlemagne n'er(cs) guariz ne tensez.
être.tr: Will not be protected nor defended by Charlemagne.

être.of: Que estrait estes de mult grant parented.»
être.tr: Since you are descended from a very great lineage.»

être.of: Mielz est que sul moerge que tant bon chevaler.
être.tr: Better that I alone die than so many good knights.

être.of: Asemblet s'est as sarrazins messag[es].
être.tr: He has joined the Saracen messengers;

être.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Merveilus hom est Charles,
être.tr: Blancandrin said: «Charles is a marvelous man,

être.of: Guenes respunt: «Itels est sis curages,
être.tr: Ganelon answers: «Such is his temper,

être.of: Jamais n'ert hume ki encuntre lui vaille.» AOI.
être.tr: Never will there be a man who can match himself against him.» AOI.

être.tr: Blancandrin said: «The Franks are very noble men;

être.of: Seit, ki l'ociet, tute pais puis avriúmes.» AOI.
être.tr: Were someone to slay him, we should then have full peace.» AOI.

être.of: Dist Blancandrins: «Mult est pesmes Rollant,
être.tr: Blancandrin said: «Roland is most fearsome,

être.of: Que il querreient, que Rollant fust ocis.
être.tr: That they would seek a way for Roland to be killed.

être.of: Esvolupet fut d'un palie alexandrin:
être.tr: It was wrapped in a silk cloth of Alexandria:

être.of: La fut li reis ki tute Espaigne tint;
être.tr: There was the king who held all Spain;

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Mahun
être.tr: And said to the king: «Be saved by Mahomet

être.tr: Who is from France, and he is a very mighty man:

être.of: Mais li quens Guenes se fut ben purpenset.
être.tr: But Count Ganelon had thought it over well.

être.of: E dist al rei: «Salvez seiez de Deu
être.tr: And said to the king: «May you be saved by God,
